focused_output: For 100 lookups, linear list membership gives O(n) construction plus 100·O(n) lookup work, while the set gives expected O(n) construction plus 100·O(1) lookup work.
Because 100 is fixed, both construction-inclusive totals simplify to O(n); these asymptotic facts alone do not establish a concrete runtime winner.
The set's per-lookup advantage depends on the expected hashing model. Its stated worst-case lookup is O(n), so that advantage is not guaranteed.
No constant factors or crossover threshold are supplied, so no stronger performance conclusion is supported.
